task.title: Read and act on a validation report
task.shortdesc: A validation report shows which rules your file passes or fails, the severity of each failure, and a plain-English mitigation message that tells you exactly what to fix. This topic walks you through reading the report and taking action.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Learn how to interpret a markdown-validator report, distinguish Required from Suggested failures, and fix the most common front-matter and body errors.

step.cmd: Look at the score line at the bottom of the report.

info.p: The score shows how many rules passed (4) out of the total (6). FAILED means at least one Required rule failed. PASSED means all Required rules passed (even if some Suggested rules failed).

step.cmd: Find every line that starts with ✗.

info.p: These lines identify the rules your file failed. Each failure shows the rule ID in square brackets, the rule name, and a mitigation message on the next line:
info.codeblock: ✗ [4] description-must-exist → Add a description field to the front matter.

info.p: Required failures show [Required] in the report. If the report does not show a level label, the rule defaults to Required. Fix all Required failures before pushing your branch.

step.cmd: Fix front-matter failures by editing the YAML block at the top of your file.

info.p: The most common front-matter failures and their fixes:

row.entry: A required key (such as description) is absent
Add the field to the YAML block: description: "Brief description here."
ms.topic is set to an unexpected value
Change the value to match the expected one shown in the mitigation message
Update ms.date to today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format
Title does not start with the required prefix
Edit the title field to match the pattern in the mitigation message

step.cmd: Fix body failures by editing the Markdown content below the front matter.

info.p: The most common body failures and their fixes:

row.entry: The document has no H1 or more than one H1
Ensure exactly one line begins with a single #
H1 does not start with the required prefix (such as "Tutorial:")
Edit the H1 to match the pattern in the mitigation message
A required H2 (such as "Prerequisites") is absent
Add the required H2 section and content

step.cmd: Re-run validation to confirm all Required failures are resolved.
step.stepresult: The report shows PASSED when all Required rules pass.
